comment.body: Don't ascribe every move to being rigged. Random is the better word. There is a lot of uncertainty, therefore risk is being reduced and hence positions are getting unwound (long and short). The most liquid go first (Treasuries and Index Futures). In an iliquid market big unwinds create big moves, that is what we are seeing now. There is no point to read too much into these intraday swings.  So not every move is due to manipulation.
comment.raw_body: Don't ascribe every move to being rigged. Random is the better word. There is a lot of uncertainty, therefore risk is being reduced and hence positions are getting unwound (long and short). The most liquid go first (Treasuries and Index Futures). In an iliquid market big unwinds create big moves, that is what we are seeing now. There is no point to read too much into these intraday swings.  So not every move is due to manipulation.

comment.body: Agreed. But I guess the biggest thing that China has going for them is stability (and that will be their selling point). China might start to look like the better of two bad choices for a lot of countries.

comment.body: Right. There is a lot of subjective Foreign Policy analysis that isn’t spotless. Europe is far from being unified into joining arms with China. Germany in fact is about to go “righty” and there are plenty of countries there who are doing the same. The Western world is severely divided and I don’t see how any of the other world powers can hold a long term alliance with the level of political volatility going on. If it were the vix, it’d be trading at 65 right now 😂
